w20: Dedicated GPU-accelerated instance, optimized for large language model inference and machine learning workloads, featuring a powerful NVIDIA A100.
c150: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-1g: Accelerator-optimized server with NVIDIA A100 GPU, 12 vCPUs, 85GB RAM for AI/ML workloads.
c200: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-1g offers an accelerator-optimized configuration with an NVIDIA A100 GPU and 12 vCPUs. Ideal for demanding AI/ML, data science, and LLM inference tasks requiring significant GPU acceleration.
w150: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-1g is an Accelerator Optimized server, featuring an x86_64 Intel Xeon processor with 12 virtual CPUs across 6 physical cores operating at 2.2 GHz. It provides 85 GB of memory. The server is equipped with a single NVIDIA Tesla A100 GPU, offering 40 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is not bundled. CPU single-core performance is weak, while multi-core performance is average. Memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks is average. Notably, LLM inference speed for prompt processing and text generation using a 135M model ranks in the top tier, demonstrating strong accelerator performance. However, gzip compression and zstd decompression single-threaded performance are poor or weak. This configuration is suitable for demanding GPU-accelerated workloads such as large language model inference, data science jobs, and machine learning training.
